items.title: Bitcoin Optech Newsletter #408
items.desc: This week’s newsletter summarizes ideas to make BIP324 transport encryption quantum secure and describes a proposal to standardize QR-based signing payloads for miniscript wallets. Also included are our regular sections summarizing proposals and discussion about changing Bitcoin’s consensus rules, announcing new releases and release candidates, and describing notable changes to popular Bitcoin infrastructure software.

items.title: Making stablecoins stable(r): can regulation help?
items.desc: by Tirupam Goel, Ulf Lewrick, Isha AgarwalWe model a stablecoin issuer that optimises capital, cash and bond holdings under persistent stablecoin flows. Absent regulation, the issuer holds little capital and favours interest-bearing but less-liquid bonds over cash.

items.title: Arthur Hayes dumps zcash holdings after Orchard Pool vulnerability revealed
items.desc: Hayes said he would reconsider his stance if his assumptions that an exploit is still possible prove to be incorrect.

items.title: Zcash selloff extends past 50% amid bug disclosure as liquidations top $100 million
items.desc: ZEC crashed over 50% to around $310 amid Zcash's Orchard bug disclosure and more than $116 million in 24-hour liquidations.

items.title: Pump.fun's Latest Experiment Is Already Getting Weird
items.desc: Pump.fun's GO bounty platform already has hundreds of listings following its pitch to let users “Pay ANYONE to do ANYTHING.”

items.title: Bitcoin sentiment hit peak bearishness at recent lows, peak bullishness near tops
items.desc: Your day-ahead look for June 5, 2026

items.title: Forward Industries moves $32M in SOL amid $1B paper loss
items.desc: Forward Industries moved $31.9 million in SOL to Coinbase Prime as its Solana bet sits over 70% underwater, underscoring growing strain on corporate crypto treasuries.

items.title: South Korea police probe Polymarket users over illegal gambling claims: Report
items.desc: South Korean police reportedly launched the country’s first illegal gambling probe into local Polymarket users amid election-betting scrutiny.

items.title: ZEC Crashes 38% as Zcash Discloses ‘Critical Counterfeiting Vulnerability’
items.desc: An Orchard vulnerability that allowed undetectable counterfeiting of ZEC in its shielded pool has reignited debate over privacy coins.

items.title: Scotland Enterprise invests £3.18 million in fintech innovation lab
items.desc: The Financial Regulation Innovation Lab (FRIL) has been granted £3.18 million from Scottish Enterprise to encourage fintech growth and collaboration.

items.title: Hong Kong taps JPMorgan, HSBC for expert group to scale tokenized bonds
items.desc: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority announced the formation of an expert group to advance the development of tokenized bonds.

items.title: JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citi to start blockchain offensive with shared tokenized network
items.desc: American's biggest banks plan to introduce a shared tokenized network next year to tackle the potential threat of stablecoins eating into their deposits.

items.title: U.S. bitcoin, ether ETFs end record multibillion outflow streak
items.desc: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs pulled in a net $3.05 million on Thursday, capping 13 straight sessions of outflows totaling roughly $4.4 billion. Ether ETFs ended a 17-day outflow streak.

items.title: Here's what could happen if bitcoin breaks below $60,000
items.desc: A confluence of factors makes $60,000 as key level to watch for in the near term, according to Deribit.

items.title: Crypto billionaires bankroll Nigel Farage's pro-crypto party
items.desc: Tether-linked billionaire Christopher Harborne and BitMEX co-founder Ben Delo donated $9.4 million to Nigel Farage’s Reform UK in the first quarter.

items.title: Strategy’s leveraged Bitcoin model has faced its first stress test: Grayscale
items.desc: “Less Bitcoin on levered DAT balance sheets and more on diversified corporate balance sheets will be a positive,” said Grayscale’s head of research, Zach Pandl.

items.title: What Is an XRP ETF? A Complete Guide to XRP Exchange-Traded Funds
items.desc: An XRP ETF is an exchange-traded fund that tracks the price of XRP. These funds trade on regulated stock exchanges such as the New York Stock Exchange or Nasdaq. Instead of buying XRP on a crypto exchange and holding it in a digital wallet, investors purchase ETF shares through a standard brokerage account. XRP is […]

items.title: Zcash plummets 38% as Shielded Labs reveals a major bug that went undetected for four years
items.desc: Shielded Labs revealed that the bug could have helped an attacker print unlimited counterfeit tokens. That could have damaged trust in the token's supply and its value.

items.title: Senate Republicans push finance watchdogs to clarify crypto capital rules
items.desc: Senator Cynthia Lummis has led a group of lawmakers urging financial regulators for “fair capital treatment for on-balance sheet treatment of digital assets.”

items.title: JPMorgan, Citi-backed consortium plans to launch tokenized deposit network in early 2027: WSJ
items.desc: The initiative would enable tokenized deposits to move instantly and support around-the-clock settlement, the Wall Street Journal reported.

items.title: Revolut co-founder Yatsenko steps down as CTO
items.desc: Revolut co-founder Vlad Yatsenko is stepping down as chief technology officer at the fintech giant and becoming a non-executive director.

items.title: JPMorgan, Citi and Big Banks Plan New Tokenized Deposit System to Answer Crypto
items.desc: The new network could help banks contend with a wave of new competition from stablecoins and crypto firms.

items.title: Bitcoin’s Pullback Tests Institutional Adoption Narrative as Pompliano Stays Bullish
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin’s Pullback Tests Institutional Adoption Narrative as Pompliano Stays Bullish Bitcoin’s recent pullback is testing the institutional-adoption thesis, but Anthony Pompliano argues the decline reflects normal capital rotation and Bitcoin’s maturation into a mainstream financial asset. This post Bitcoin’s Pullback Tests Institutional Adoption Narrative as Pompliano Stays Bullish first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: 0x Opens Cross-Chain API to All Developers with 12 Bridge Partners on Day One
items.desc: 0x Protocol has opened its Cross-Chain API to general availability with 12 bridge providers live from day one, including Circle, LayerZero, Stargate and Across — accessible through a single API integration.

items.title: Bitcoin fell 21% after Strategy’s debt buyback news: Is Terra Luna-style doom loop next?
items.desc: Bitcoin price collapsed as Strategy faced tighter liquidity conditions and paused its BTC buying. Is it time to jump ship or buy the dip?

items.title: Russia targets British 17-year-old for alleging digital assets were skirting sanctions
items.desc: Political activist Bill Browder, the teenager’s father, said his son was “the first high school student in the world to be sanctioned by an authoritarian regime” over a report on the ruble-pegged stablecoin A7A5.

items.title: White House crypto adviser Witt defends Clarity Act, calls it ‘pro-law enforcement’ as lawmakers race to pass bill
items.desc: The White House's top cryptocurrency adviser, Patrick Witt, called the Clarity Act a "pro-enforcement bill," despite pushback.

items.title: 'Looksmaxxing' Trend Spawns $100M Gray Market Fueled By Bitcoin, Stablecoins: Chainalysis
items.desc: Demand for peptides fueled by the "looksmaxxing" trend has spawned a $100 million gray market paid for primarily with crypto.

items.title: Professional investors dumped 52K BTC worth of ETFs in Q1, filings show
items.desc: US spot Bitcoin ETF ownership shifted during the market downturn as hedge funds exited positions, while banks and long-term allocators continued building exposure.

items.title: Schwab Strategist: Bitcoin’s $60,000 Mining Cost Could Mark the Cycle Bottom
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Schwab Strategist: Bitcoin’s $60,000 Mining Cost Could Mark the Cycle Bottom Bitcoin's recent crash may have found a durable bottom near $60,000 because that level roughly matches the production cost of the most efficient miners, creating an energy-based floor. This post Schwab Strategist: Bitcoin’s $60,000 Mining Cost Could Mark the Cycle Bottom first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Crypto Clarity Act in spotlight for bad-actor provisions as Senate process grinds forward
items.desc: The industry has been trying to make a case this week the Clarity Act provides law enforcement strong tools to combat illicit finance involving cryptocurrency.

items.title: Coinbase to launch token-backed mortgage down payments this summer
items.desc: A Coinbase-Better Home & Finance initiative will allow qualified borrowers to use Bitcoin and USDC as collateral for home loan down payments.

items.title: Bitcoin tumbles after Strategy sale unnerves crypto traders
items.desc: Cryptocurrency is heading for biggest weekly loss since November 2022

items.title: Bitcoin Privacy in 2026: A Practical Guide
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Privacy in 2026: A Practical Guide Bitcoin remains pseudonymous by design, but real-world threats from exchanges and data leaks demand stronger protections. Experts highlight Sparrow Wallet, Bisq, and Boltz as key 2026 solutions for on-chain and off-chain privacy. This post Bitcoin Privacy in 2026: A Practical Guide first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.

items.title: Bitcoin bulls' fate rests on $60K support as crypto erases $2T in market cap
items.desc: Bitcoin continued "incredible" 2022 bear market repeat behavior as sellers kept control over BTC price rebound attempts.

items.title: Bybit joins Western Union’s new USDPT network as stablecoin expands distribution
items.desc: The exchange integration gives Western Union’s dollar-pegged token access to crypto market liquidity as payment providers accelerate their adoption of stablecoins.

items.title: Ether.fi allocates $100 million to a Plume RWA vault, giving users access to yield
items.desc: The allocation comes from a mix of ether.fi's liquidity provider base and managed capital from its existing liquid vaults.

items.title: Chainalysis says ‘top-tier’ gray market peptide vendors turn to bitcoin and stablecoins
items.desc: “To scale, the gray-market peptide trade adopted cryptocurrency as its backbone,” Chainalysis said in a report.

items.title: ERC-3643: The Case for Permissioned Tokens for Insitutional Adoption
items.desc: What if the blockchain token you hold doesn't actually give you ownership of the underlying asset? Dennis O'Connell of the ERC-3643 Association joins Converge to expose the hidden differences between tokenization standards — and why knowing which one you're buying matters more than most people

items.title: The BRCA is ready to become law
items.desc: The BRCA’s final text reflects the concerns of skeptics while preserving the protection non-custodial developers need. The post The BRCA is ready to become law appeared first on Coin Center.

items.title: JPMorgan says crypto bill may have only a narrow window for passage this year
items.desc: JPMorgan analysts said the crypto market structure bill, or Clarity Act, may have only a narrow window for passage this year.

items.title: DOJ Task Force Freezes $3.8M in Illicit Crypto—With Help From Coinbase, SpaceX and Meta
items.desc: Some of America's biggest companies helped squash crypto fraud stemming from organized crime in Southeast Asia.

items.title: Fannie Mae-Backed Bitcoin Home Mortgages Are Finally Here, Coinbase Says
items.desc: Coinbase said a Michigan couple closed on the first-ever conventional, Fannie Mae-backed home mortgage by pledging Bitcoin as collateral.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Senate Republicans urged financial regulators to rework bank capital rules for digital assets
items.desc: A group of Senate Republicans is pressing key financial regulatory agencies to clarify capital standards for digital assets.

items.title: Robinhood enters Canada as it completes acquisition of WonderFi
items.desc: Robinhood Markets, Inc. (“Robinhood”) has closed its acquisition of WonderFi, a Canadian leader in digital asset products and services.

items.title: Western Union Deploys USDPT on Bybit's Fiat Channels in Latin America
items.desc: Western Union has made its USDPT stablecoin available on Bybit's fiat channels in Latin America, making Bybit the first major crypto exchange to integrate USDPT — arriving two days after MoneyGram launched its own rival dollar token on Stellar.

items.title: Sky Launches Fixed-Rate Yield Product Built on Pendle, Targeting $6B sUSDS Pool
items.desc: Sky (formerly MakerDAO) launched Fixed Yield on Wednesday — a term-based alternative to the variable Sky Savings Rate built on Pendle Protocol v2, giving sUSDS depositors a locked rate to a named maturity date.

items.title: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.desc: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.

items.title: Bitcoin Buys a Home: Better and Coinbase Close First Fannie Mae-Backed BTC Mortgage
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Buys a Home: Better and Coinbase Close First Fannie Mae-Backed BTC Mortgage Better and Coinbase have funded the first Fannie Mae-backed mortgage that lets homebuyers use Bitcoin as collateral for their down payment loan This post Bitcoin Buys a Home: Better and Coinbase Close First Fannie Mae-Backed BTC Mortgage first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: British Teen Sanctioned By Russia After Alleging Crypto Use to Evade Sanctions
items.desc: The teenager's research into Russia’s alleged illicit crypto flows appears to have prompted Moscow's retaliation.

items.title: Not all Ethereum layer 2s are dying, but many general-purpose chains no longer have a reason to exist
items.desc: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at the state of the Ethereum layer-2 ecosystem.

items.title: Standard Chartered Sees Bitcoin Bottom ‘Almost In’ as Sell-Off Cuts 14% in Seven Days
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Standard Chartered Sees Bitcoin Bottom ‘Almost In’ as Sell-Off Cuts 14% in Seven Days Standard Chartered's Geoff Kendrick argues Bitcoin's sharp decline—driven by Strategy's surprise sale, ETF outflows, and liquidations—may be nearing a bottom. This post Standard Chartered Sees Bitcoin Bottom ‘Almost In’ as Sell-Off Cuts 14% in Seven Days first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Apex, Archax join Goldman Sachs tokenized real estate fund project
items.desc: Apex Group is providing fund services for a tokenized real estate fund using Goldman Sachs’ GS DAP platform.

items.title: Cosmos Labs acquires block explorer Mintscan, forms Korea subsidiary to expand ecosystem
items.desc: Cosmos Labs has acquired Cosmos block explorer Mintscan and formed a new Korea-based subsidiary, Cosmos Labs Korea.

items.title: Triple-A puts multicurrency account on stablecoin rails
items.desc: Today, Triple-A, a licensed global payment institution, announces the launch of the European rollout of stablecoin-enabled Multicurrency Accounts, giving global businesses access to local banking collection rails without the need to set up a local entity.

items.title: Morning Minute: Crypto Crashes, New Lows In Sight
items.desc: Bitcoin falls to $62k and Arthur Hayes selling his HYPE and NEAR position leads to double digit fallout for recent alt darlings.

items.title: ‘When the market offers discounts, we lean in’: DDC Enterprise lifts bitcoin holdings to 2,804 BTC
items.desc: DDC Enterprise added 90 bitcoin, raising its total holdings to 2,804 BTC and reaching 28th among public corporate holders.

items.title: Visa and Brale Test Privacy-Enabled SBC Stablecoin Settlement on Canton Network
items.desc: Visa and stablecoin issuer Brale are piloting settlement using SBC, a U.S. dollar-backed stablecoin, on Canton Network, the privacy-preserving institutional DLT, testing whether blockchain settlement can meet bank-grade compliance requirements.

items.title: Russia sanctions British teenager for alleging A7A5 use in funding Ukraine war
items.desc: The A7A5 stablecoin was designed to bypass sanctions imposed on Russia following its invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

items.title: Bitcoin price just tagged 200-week trend line that defined 2022 bear market
items.desc: Bitcoin brought back the trend line that functioned as resistance in the 2022 bear market, with BTC price RSI approaching its lowest in six years.

items.title: FG Nexus offloads additional $17.8M Ether as losses top $100M
items.desc: FG Nexus offloads another 10,000 ETH, pushing realized and paper losses above $100 million as the Ethereum treasury firm continues cutting exposure.

items.title: Polymarket users cry foul after Strategy sale market resolves to ‘no’
items.desc: A Polymarket contract on whether Strategy sold Bitcoin by May 31 resolved to no after traders disputed how the sale should count.

items.title: Standard Chartered's three 'Ifs' that stand between bitcoin and a market low
items.desc: Your day-ahead look for June 4, 2026

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs bleed $4.4B as outflow run extends to 13 trading days
items.desc: US-listed spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $397 million in outflows on Wednesday, extending a 13-day red streak to $4.4 billion as Bitcoin has fallen about 21% since May 15.

items.title: JPMorgan warns time is running short for crypto market structure bill
items.desc: The Clarity Act still faces several legislative hurdles, and disputes over stablecoin yield have emerged as a key sticking point, the bank said.

items.title: Tom Lee’s BitMine Plans $300M Preferred Stock Sale for ETH Treasury Push
items.desc: The offering would tie fixed cash dividends to a staking-heavy ETH treasury model as Strategy’s preferred stock remains under pressure.

items.title: Apex Group suports launch of tokenised real estate fund
items.desc: Apex Group Ltd ("Apex Group"), a global financial services provider with over $3.5 trillion in assets serviced, today announced its role in the launch of a blockchain-native real estate fund developed in collaboration with Archax, Goldman Sachs, LRC Group, and Ownera.

items.title: Over $600M in Bitcoin longs liquidated as BTC dips toward $60K
items.desc: Many traders expect Bitcoin to stage a short-term relief bounce toward $70,000, but the broader technical setup remains bearish for BTC.

items.title: US House Democrats call for FTC probe into prediction markets
items.desc: The lawmakers are also asking the FTC for information on whether it has plans to take investigative or enforcement action against prediction markets for possible deceptive practices.

items.title: Bitmine eyes dividend-paying preferred shares, echoing Strategy’s playbook
items.desc: The move comes as Ether prices slumped to a 14-month low below $1,750 on Thursday.

items.title: Coinbase freezes $3M tied to Southeast Asia crypto fraud networks
items.desc: Authorities around the world have been heavily targeting scam infrastructure this year, with joint actions involving the US, UAE, China, Austria and Albania.

items.title: CFTC follows SEC in scrapping ‘no-deny’ policy for settlements
items.desc: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig says the rescission of its “no-deny” policy means it now has more flexibility when settling enforcement actions.

items.title: Cardano Slumps to 5-Year Low Price as Charles Hoskinson Warns of 'Wave of Failures'
items.desc: Founder Charles Hoskinson was brutally honest about the troubles the Cardano ecosystem could face as the market continues declining.

items.title: Deel Deploys Stripe's Full Stablecoin Stack to Pay 1.5M Contractors in DLUSD
items.desc: Deel is the first enterprise to combine Bridge, Privy, and Tempo in a single product, issuing a custom USD-denominated stablecoin for 1.5 million contractors across 150 countries, starting in Argentina.

items.title: PENDLE Token Goes Live on Revolut, Reaching 20M EEA Crypto Traders
items.desc: The yield-tokenization protocol's $233M-mcap token is now buyable inside Revolut's MiCA-licensed crypto app across the UK, EU, Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein — the second on-chain yield protocol to land on the fintech's regulated rails after Maple's SYRUP in April.

items.title: Tether Debuts Tokenized Gold Stablecoin Visa Card That Pays Out Crypto Rewards
items.desc: Holders will be able to do more with their gold, instantly spending Tether's tokenized version anywhere Visa is accepted.

items.title: Franklin Templeton CEO: Blockchains Threaten Wall Street’s Fee Machine, Not Its Technology
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Franklin Templeton CEO: Blockchains Threaten Wall Street’s Fee Machine, Not Its Technology Franklin Templeton CEO Jenny Johnson said traditional finance resists public blockchains because they threaten fee-based revenue, as the firm expands aggressively into tokenization, bitcoin products, and on-chain finance. This post Franklin Templeton CEO: Blockchains Threaten Wall Street’s Fee Machine, Not Its Technology first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Someone Just Redeemed a 15-Year-Old Physical Bitcoin, Scoring $1.78 Million in BTC
items.desc: Physical Casascius coins were minted 15 years ago in the early days of Bitcoin—and the BTC held within is worth a whole lot more today.

items.title: Revolut Plans 2027 US Bank Launch With Stablecoin Services Built In From Day One
items.desc: The British neobank, with 70 million customers and a $75 billion valuation, will pair FDIC-insured accounts with stablecoin access on a single platform — a tier-one fintech entering US banking with stablecoins designed in, not bolted on.

items.title: Builder-Deployed Perp Markets Push Hyperliquid to Record Share of Global Perps Volume
items.desc: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 framework — which lets anyone deploy a perpetual futures market on the protocol's settlement layer — cleared $62 billion in May volume, pushing the venue to a record 6.63% share of global perps and 14.4% against Binance.

items.title: World Cup Crypto Scams Are Targeting Soccer Fans, Law Enforcement Warns
items.desc: Law enforcement agencies are warning soccer fans of fake ticket sites and crypto payment schemes around the World Cup.

items.title: Mastercard Expands Stablecoin Settlement via Circle's USDC, Ripple's RLUSD and Beyond
items.desc: Mastercard said it's deepening its commitment to the "always-on" economy, buffing out its stablecoin settlement capabilities.

items.title: Bitcoin Slides Below $66,000 as Spot ETF Outflow Streak Hits a Record 12 Days
items.desc: US spot Bitcoin ETFs pulled in another $519 million of net redemptions on June 2, extending the longest outflow run since the funds launched and tipping a leveraged market into $1.86 billion of forced selling.

items.title: Stripe Millionaire Loses Bid for Congress to Candidate Backed by Ripple Co-Founder
items.desc: Former Stripe engineer Saikat Chakrabarti lost his bid to succeed Nancy Pelosi in California to a candidate backed by Ripple's Chris Larsen.

items.title: Cardano's TapTools Winding Down is a Symptom of a Shrinking Chain
items.desc: TapTools, the most-used analytics platform on Cardano, said on X it will wind down within two weeks after losing its fifth senior executive in a year. Founder Charles Hoskinson warned in a video the same day that more older Cardano-ecosystem projects will fold in the second half of 2026.

items.title: Old DxSale Lockers Drained for $7.3M Across 1,400 BNB Chain Pools as Owner-Privilege Exploits Pile Up
items.desc: A dormant launchpad contract from 2021 was emptied this week through a quiet ownership transfer and a one-wei fee reset — the latest in a string of BNB Chain drains that turn admin keys into the attack surface.

items.title: Mastercard Opens Card-Settlement Network on Eight Blockchains, Adding Weekend and Holiday Cycles
items.desc: Issuers and acquirers can now clear card transactions in regulated stablecoins on Arbitrum, Base, Canton, Ethereum, Polygon, Solana, Tempo and XRPL, with ARQ, CBW Bank, Cross River, Lead Bank and Nuvei lined up as the first adopters in the US and Latin America.

items.title: Binance Closes Its Centralized NFT Marketplace, Joining Coinbase, Kraken in CEX-Backed NFT Retreat
items.desc: The world's largest crypto exchange is folding its in-house NFT service into Binance Wallet by July 3, ending the centralized-marketplace bet a year after Coinbase NFT shut down and four months after X2Y2's sunset — a near-complete unwinding of the CEX-backed NFT marketplace category.

items.title: 160 Officials Tell Senate to Pass CLARITY Act as Floor Talks Resume
items.desc: A Blockchain Association letter signed by ex-CIA, DOJ, Treasury, FBI, IRS-CI, Secret Service and military officials lands on Thune and Schumer the same day the Senate restarts crypto market-structure negotiations.
